narrative.headline: 2018 GMC Acadia: power train and electrical drive most owner complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 383 complaints with NHTSA. The power train is by far the biggest concern at 42% of reports, tied to 4 crashes and 4 injuries. Electrical problems come next at 28%, then engine at 8%, steering at 6%, and brakes at 5%. One electrical report involved a fire. No deaths were reported.

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: The top complaint area by a wide margin: 159 reports (42%). Linked to 4 crashes and 4 injuries, so test how the car shifts and drives carefully before buying.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: Second most reported: 108 reports (28%), tied to 3 crashes, 3 injuries, and 1 fire. Check that all electronics work and watch for any burning smell.

issues.sample: TL* THE CONTACT RENTED A 2018 GMC ACADIA. WHILE THE CONTACT'S FRIEND WAS DRIVING 40 MPH, THE ENGINE REVVED AND THE VEHICLE  ACCELERATED ON ITS OWN. THE BRAKES FAILED TO STOP THE VEHICLE. AS A RESULT, THE VEHICLE DROVE THROUGH A PARTITION AND CRASHED INTO A BRICK WALL. THE AIR BAG
issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: 31 reports (8%), but serious: tied to 4 crashes and 5 injuries. Worth a close look despite the lower count.

issues.title: Steering
issues.blurb: 23 reports (6%), tied to 2 crashes and 5 injuries. Test the steering through full turns and watch for any power-steering warning.

issues.title: Service Brakes
issues.blurb: Smaller count but the most injuries: 21 reports (5%), tied to 8 injuries and 4 crashes. Inspect the brakes carefully before buying.
